zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de Barton de Wallop於1290年出生在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓時，他的父親William De Burton35歲，母親Joane Denmede31歲。他於1320年與Lady Alice Hughes在英國英格蘭維爾爾卡爾內結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1346年在英國英格蘭哈普爾瑟 瓦洛普享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard de Barton de Wallop于1290年出生在英国英格兰哈普爾素坦普頓时，他的父亲William De Burton35岁，母亲Joane Denmede31岁。他于1320年与Lady Alice Hughes在英国英格兰維爾爾卡爾內结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1346年在英国英格兰哈普爾瑟 瓦洛普享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Barton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called with Old English bere or bær ‘barley’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’, i.e. an outlying grange. Compare Barwick . The name is also found in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as de Bartún.

Polish (Bartoń); Czech and Slovak (mainly Bartoň): from a pet form of the personal name Polish Bartłomiej, Czech Bartoloměj, Slovak Bartolomej, from Latin Bartholomaeus (see Bartholomew ). This surname is also found in Germany.

variants.name: Barwick
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Berwick or Barwick, for example in Northumberland, Norfolk, Somerset, and Yorkshire, from Old English bere ‘barley’ + wīc ‘outlying farm building’, i.e. a granary lying some distance away from the main settlement.
North German: habitational name from a place called Berwick, near Soest, in Westphalia.

nameMeanings.name: Wallop
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Nether and Over Wallop (Hants).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de Barton de Wallop was born in 1290, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William De Burton, was 35 and his mother, Joane Denmede, was 31. He married Lady Alice Hughes in 1320, in Calne, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1346, in Nether Wallop, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56.

Il a épousé Lady Alice Hughes en 1320, à Calne, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1346, à Nether Wallop, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 56 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Barton de Wallop(이)가 1290년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William De Burton(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Joane Denmede(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1320년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 캘니에서 Lady Alice Hughes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1346년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 네터 월로프에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de Barton de Wallop родился в 1290 г. в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William De Burton было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Joane Denmede, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alice Hughes в 1320 г. в местности калн, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1346 г. в местности нетир валлоп, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 56 лет.
